name: Zinc-Responsive Dermatosis
description: Zinc-responsive dermatosis manifests as skin crusting and hair loss, primarily around the muzzle, eyes, ears, and footpads. It occurs either due to reduced intestinal zinc absorption (Syndrome I in Nordic breeds) or feeding zinc-deficient diets (Syndrome II).
symptoms: Crusting and scaling around face and footpads
treatment_overview: Daily zinc supplementation (zinc sulphate or zinc methionine) resolves Syndrome I rapidly. Syndrome II resolves by correcting the diet. Lifelong supplementation is needed for Syndrome I.
prevention_tips: Do not supplement calcium excessively (impairs zinc absorption)
Nordic breeds may need lifelong zinc supplementation
